description_long: This is a classic claw hammer featuring a robust, dark metallic head and a long, slightly curved wooden handle. The head has a flat striking face on one side for driving nails and a pronounced, bifurcated claw on the opposite side for prying and removing nails. The handle appears to be made of solid wood, showing a natural grain and a slightly weathered texture, providing a comfortable grip. The metal head is matte black, with a smooth finish on the striking face and a more angular, functional design on the claw. No visible branding or decorative elements are present, emphasizing its utilitarian purpose. The hammer shows no obvious signs of wear, damage, or additional attachments.
description: A traditional claw hammer with a dark metal head and a long, natural wood handle, designed for driving and removing nails.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat striking face and the full width of the claw, with the handle extending backward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the wooden handle and the profile of the claw and striking face.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the handle's end and the open, curved shape of the claw.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the handle's length and the hammer head's profile, including the striking face and claw.
attribute_handle_type: Wooden straight handle with a natural grain, slightly curved for ergonomic grip, and a matte, textured finish.
attribute_head_type: Claw hammer head with a flat striking face and a bifurcated, curved claw for nail removal, made of dark steel.
